description: The venv was broken (created with path /Users/frakbox/trading_bot2/venv which doesn't exist). Had to recreate venv and install all packages manually. Took ~15 minutes of debugging.
potential_fix: In CLAUDE.md or smoke_test.py, add a venv path check and auto-recreate if broken. Or include a requirements.txt with all specific pinned versions that work together (alpaca-trade-api has strict conflicts with urllib3/websockets).

description: alpaca-trade-api 3.2.0 has very strict dependency requirements (msgpack==1.0.3, PyYAML==6.0.1, websockets<11) that conflict with yfinance requirements. Had to install with --no-deps and then add missing packages one by one.
potential_fix: Pin all dependency versions in requirements.txt. Consider replacing alpaca-trade-api with alpaca-py (the newer SDK that doesn't have these conflicts). File: requirements.txt

description: measure_event_impact() returns flat keys like avg_abnormal_1d, not nested dict like result['1d']. Had to inspect the return structure before using it. CLAUDE.md API quick reference is misleading - shows nested dict syntax that doesn't match actual API.
potential_fix: Update CLAUDE.md API Quick Reference to show actual flat key structure: result['avg_abnormal_1d'] not result['1d']['mean_abnormal_return_pct']. Or add a convenience wrapper.

description: measure_event_impact individual_impacts don't include the date field - shows '?' for date. Makes it impossible to cross-reference events with their dates without re-running.
potential_fix: Fix market_data.py to include the event date in individual_impacts dict. Should be trivial to add 'date' key to the per-event result.

description: yfinance returns empty data for delisted tickers (SESN, ATNX, ICPT, CORV). These are the most impactful FDA CRL events. No fallback data source available in the codebase. Caused 33% data quality failure in FDA backtest.
potential_fix: Add Polygon.io or Tiingo API as fallback data source for delisted tickers. Both have historical data for stocks that were delisted/acquired. Add to market_data.py as a secondary data source when yfinance returns no data.

description: Wikipedia S&P 500 component changes table returned 403 Forbidden. Needed to manually search for deletion dates across multiple press releases. Wikipedia would have been a single comprehensive source.
potential_fix: Use S&P Global press release archive (press.spglobal.com) as primary source - it's structured and fetchable. Could build a scraper that finds all S&P 500 rebalance announcements by pattern-matching press release URLs.

description: yfinance does not preserve historical price data for delisted tickers. ~40% of SEC Form 4 cluster events from 2020-2022 have no retrievable price data because the companies have since been acquired, gone private, or gone bankrupt. This creates systematic survivorship bias in any backtest using this data source.
potential_fix: Use a commercial data provider (Polygon.io, Quandl, CRSP) that stores historical prices for delisted tickers. Alternatively, use WRDS for academic access to CRSP which has all historical prices. Short-term workaround: filter to large-cap tickers only (use a market cap threshold or S&P 1500 membership list).

description: The create_hypothesis() function signature requires out_of_sample_split with validation_indices populated before running any backtest. For true pre-registration (before any data), had to use literature-anchor placeholders. This creates a mismatch between the pre-registration and the actual backtest. The function should support a 'pre_registration_only' mode that defers OOS split to after data collection.
potential_fix: Add a pre_registration_only=True parameter to create_hypothesis() that relaxes the OOS split requirement, logging it as a forward-looking commitment. OOS split is then provided when the backtest is run.

description: Batch summary outputs from measure_event_impact show averages contaminated by a few extreme outliers in the batch - this made it appear batches had 400%+ returns. Required post-hoc outlier detection and removal. The individual_impacts list was clean but the batch-level print was misleading.
potential_fix: measure_event_impact batch summary should winsorize at 3-sigma before printing avg_abnormal_Nd, or flag when mean is pulled by outliers

description: record_literature() takes a dict not a string, but the CLAUDE.md API reference shows it as record_literature(event_type, findings) suggesting string. Had to read source to discover dict requirement.
potential_fix: Update CLAUDE.md API reference to show dict signature for record_literature()

description: create_hypothesis() has a different signature than the CLAUDE.md quick reference suggests (uses description=, mechanism= shorthand). Had to read source code to get actual parameter names.
potential_fix: Update CLAUDE.md to show actual parameter names for create_hypothesis()

description: yfinance fails on ~30-40% of insider cluster events due to delisted tickers (acquired, failed, or ticker changed). Small sample (n=61) backtests give noisy results. Full population (n=1452) runs take ~30 min. No solution yet for real-time delisted ticker price data.
potential_fix: Add Tiingo or Polygon API for delisted ticker historical prices. Alternatively, pre-filter events to large-cap S&P 500 components where delistment rate is near zero.

description: SEC quarterly bulk ZIP data (used by insider_cluster_detector.py) is released 1-2 months after quarter end. Q1 2026 data not available until May 2026. Cannot backfill recent insider cluster events for live trading using the bulk data approach.
potential_fix: Built tools/realtime_insider_scanner.py using EDGAR quarterly form.idx (updated daily). This solves the lag. But parsing individual XML files for each CIK is slow (~100 CIKs/minute). Could be sped up with async requests.

description: realtime_insider_scanner.py: SEC EDGAR rate limiting caused ReadTimeout when parsing Form 4 XML files. 10-second timeout too short; need retry logic with rate limiting. Fixed by adding sec_get() helper with 3 retries, exponential backoff, and 0.35s delay between requests.
potential_fix: Added sec_get() wrapper to realtime_insider_scanner.py with proper rate limiting and retry. Also consider async requests (aiohttp) for 5-10x speedup.

description: OpenInsider.com cluster screener requires HTML parsing with ticker extraction from JavaScript onmouseover strings. The data is embedded in complex HTML rather than being a clean API. Had to use regex to extract tickers from href attributes. No official API exists. Took 3-4 attempts to get the right parsing approach.
potential_fix: Build a dedicated openinsider_scraper.py tool in tools/ that correctly parses the cluster screener and outputs clean JSON. Cache results for 1 hour. Could also check if OpenInsider has an undocumented CSV export endpoint.

description: realtime_insider_scanner.py with --max-check 300-500 runs longer than 90s timeout. The tool is too slow for interactive use - SEC EDGAR XML parsing at 0.35s/request means 300 CIKs = 105+ seconds. Had to abandon scanner and use OpenInsider.com scraping instead.
potential_fix: Either: (1) Reduce --max-check to 50-100 for quick scan, (2) Add async requests (aiohttp) for 5-10x speedup, (3) Prioritize high-cap tickers only (EDGAR company search can filter by size). The current tool is only practical as a batch overnight job.

description: Built tools/largecap_filter.py to address recurring yfinance delistment failures (entries 5, 7, 12). Tool caches market caps and filters events to >500M or >5B tickers. 2023 sample: 34% pass 500M filter, 20% are unknown/delisted.
potential_fix: RESOLVED: Use filter_to_largecap() before building event_dates list for backtests. Add to standard backtest workflow in CLAUDE.md.

description: FDA CRL dates in knowledge_base.json from previous session were wrong: APLT date was Feb 14 2024 (actual Nov 27 2024), ARDX date was Jul 29 2021 (actual Jul 19 2021), OTLK date was Jul 26 2023 (actual Aug 29 2023). Wrong dates led to no-event measurements (+18% and +19% returns where crashes should show). Required manual verification via yfinance + web search.
potential_fix: When recording CRL dates from knowledge base, always verify by checking yfinance for a big volume/move around that date before using in backtest. Add a date_verification step to the FDA backtest workflow.

description: ALDX Mar 17 2026 CRL: stock opened at $1.12 (from $4.23 prior close = -73%), but then RECOVERED to $1.81 by Mar 19. 3d return from open = +64.8%. This is unusual - initial gap then sharp recovery. Contaminated the clinical CRL dataset. Need to classify: was this pre-anticipated? No - the stock was at $4.23 suggesting surprise. Recovery may be due to buy-the-dip after extreme selloff, or short-squeeze.
potential_fix: Add a 'was_pre_anticipated' flag to clinical CRL events (check: did stock decline >20% in week before CRL? If yes, likely pre-anticipated). This would help classify ALDX correctly.

description: Multiple qualifying CRL events (SESN Aug 2021, TCDA Aug 2020, YMAB Dec 2022, ORPH Jun 2021) are delisted and data is unavailable via both yfinance and Tiingo. This is the main bottleneck preventing N from reaching 6+ for hypothesis pre-registration. The events clearly existed (confirmed via web search with large percentage crashes) but price history is inaccessible.
potential_fix: Try Polygon.io API (free tier has historical data for delisted tickers). Alternatively, use EDGAR XBRL financial data or press releases to extract price information. Or use 10-Q/10-K filings which often mention stock price ranges. Another option: look for surviving tickers that acquired these companies (e.g., Sesen Bio was acquired by Cabaletta Bio - see if historical data transferred).

description: FDA CRL post-crash continuation research stuck at N=4. 3 next candidates (IRON, SYDNEXIS, LIB Therapeutics) are all private companies with no stock ticker. BioPharmCatalyst historical PDUFA data behind paywall. Main bottleneck: delisted tickers + private companies. SESN (Sesen Bio, Aug 2021) would be the 5th event but is delisted and has no price data on yfinance or Tiingo free tier.
potential_fix: Use Polygon.io Basic plan ($29/mo) for delisted ticker historical prices. Alternatively, SEC 8-K filings show the stock trading range immediately after CRL (Item 8.01 8-Ks sometimes mention stock price impact). Could also check IndiePharma or StatNews FDA CRL archives for more events 2016-2019.

description: Phase 2/3 clinical trial failure events not easily searchable. SEC EDGAR 8-K full-text search returns 100+ results per year but includes large pharma (not qualifying). FDA CRL API only covers NDA-stage CRLs, not Phase 2/3 failures. Need a dedicated clinical trial failure database (ClinicalTrials.gov results registry, or BioPharma Catalyst). Most qualifying Phase 2/3 failures have delisted tickers or no price data.
potential_fix: Build a tool that: (1) queries ClinicalTrials.gov API for trials with status=Completed AND results.completion_status=Primary_endpoint_failed AND phase=Phase2|Phase3 AND sponsor_type=small_biotech, (2) matches the compound to a stock ticker via company name, (3) checks yfinance for crash data. ClinicalTrials.gov API is free and covers 2005-present.

description: Previous session recorded WRONG event dates for FDA CRL events. Required re-verification of all 5 events via price data lookup + web search. Wasted 8-10 turns verifying/correcting.
potential_fix: Build a verify_event_date() tool that: (1) takes ticker + approximate date, (2) downloads price history around that date, (3) finds the actual >30% crash day within +/- 5 trading days, (4) returns the verified crash date and pre-crash close price. This would prevent date errors.

description: FDA PDUFA calendar not accessible via web. biopharmcatalyst.com returns 403, raps.org returns 404, fdanewscalendar.com refused connection. ClinicalTrials.gov API returns 400 for complex queries. Cannot find upcoming qualifying events to monitor for d302c84b hypothesis.
potential_fix: Build a PDUFA calendar scraper from FDA's Drugs@FDA (accessdata.fda.gov) which lists PDUFA dates in structured format. Alternative: subscribe to BioPharma Catalyst email alerts or build a scraper for SEC EDGAR 8-K filings with Item 4.01 (matters submitted to vote) + biotech company filter. Or: monitor EDGAR full-text search (efts.sec.gov) for 'primary endpoint' + 'failed' in recent 8-Ks.

description: Phase 2/3 failure event dates were wrong in previous session's knowledge base. ATHX was listed as 2021-11-30 (actual crash: 2022-05-20), PRQR was listed as 2022-12-06 (actual crash: 2022-02-11). Required running verify_event_date tool on all events to find correct dates. verify_event_date tool (built this session) would have prevented this immediately.
potential_fix: RESOLVED: verify_event_date.py tool is now available. Policy: always verify Phase 2/3 failure event dates using verify_event_date before using in backtest. Also: add verification step to the research queue instructions for Phase 2/3 research.

description: edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py initially failed to extract company names and tickers because EDGAR API response uses display_names field (not entity_name or tickers). Had to inspect raw API response to fix extraction logic. Also the hit deduplication key was wrong (used accession_no not adsh field).
potential_fix: RESOLVED: Fixed edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py to use display_names[0] for ticker extraction (regex pattern for (TICKER) in string) and adsh field for deduplication.

description: research.verify_data_integrity() threw TypeError: expected string or bytes-like object when next_session_priorities contained nested dicts (task was a dict with 'task' key instead of string). The research_queue.py set_next_session_priorities() writes priorities with set_by_session wrapper but the inner task was also wrapped. Fixed in research.py line 1001 to handle nested task dict.
potential_fix: RESOLVED: Fixed in research.py to handle both string and nested-dict task formats. Also cleaned up next_session_priorities to use flat string format going forward.

description: EDGAR full-text search (efts.sec.gov) returned 0 results for SC 13D filings when searching for activist fund names with forms=SC+13D. The activists file SC 13D via many different entity names and the search doesn't match reliably. Had to use company submissions API (data.sec.gov/submissions/CIKXXXXXX.json) but that returned 0 SC 13D filings for Elliott (CIK 0001536907) - wrong CIK. Ended up with valid Starboard results only by searching full-text for Starboard name in SC 13D.
potential_fix: Build a tool that: (1) takes activist fund name, (2) does EDGAR company search to find correct CIK, (3) downloads all filings JSON, (4) filters for SC 13D. The CIK for major activists: Starboard=0001591588, Elliott=multiple entities, Icahn=multiple. Need correct CIK for each.

description: EDGAR efts.sec.gov full-text search API returns 403 to WebFetch tool but works with Python requests when User-Agent header is set. WebFetch does not set a proper User-Agent.
potential_fix: Use Python requests with User-Agent header instead of WebFetch for EDGAR API calls. Already using this approach successfully in edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py

description: No programmatic way to find specific dates of surprise large buyback announcements. Web search only returns well-known mega-cap events (Apple, MSFT, Alphabet) which are too large and too expected to have tradeable reactions. Need EDGAR 8-K Item 8.01 scanner specifically for buyback authorizations to get small/mid-cap event dates with sizes.
potential_fix: Build EDGAR 8-K scanner that (1) searches full-text for repurchase authorization language, (2) extracts dollar amount, (3) looks up market cap at that date, (4) computes buyback-as-pct-of-mcap. This would enable proper backtesting of any buyback sub-hypothesis.

description: EDGAR clinical failure scanner returns candidates requiring manual web search to qualify/disqualify. 7 candidates in March 14-21 scan required individual searches for: (1) news content, (2) crash magnitude, (3) pre-anticipation check, (4) pipeline depth, (5) repeat-event check. Took ~6 tool calls for 7 candidates.
potential_fix: Build automated qualification filter: after finding 8-K candidates, immediately fetch price data from yfinance to check >55% crash, then check 10-day prior returns for pre-anticipation. This would auto-filter ~70% of candidates without web search.

description: ALDX March 2026 CRL was the THIRD CRL for the same drug. No tool to detect whether a clinical event is a repeat attempt vs fresh failure. Had to use web search to learn about the 2023 and 2025 prior CRLs.
potential_fix: Add repeat-event detection to edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py: cross-reference each candidate against known_effects and dead_ends in knowledge_base.json to flag if same company or same drug appeared before.

description: Session starts without knowing which insider cluster opportunities were live in the past 48 hours. POOL ($3.6M, 3 insiders, filed March 17), AMRZ ($4.6M, 5 insiders, filed March 16), AHCO ($20M, 3 insiders, March 12) were all stale by session time. These are tradeable with hypothesis 1cb6140f but the 3d window had passed.
potential_fix: RESOLVED: Built tools/cluster_auto_scanner.py + launchd schedule (daily 9:15 PM ET). Scanner logs fresh clusters to research_queue.json for next LLM session to review immediately.

description: Testing 5 different signal categories (fallen angel, profit warnings, secondary offerings, IPO lockup, dividends) required manual identification of historical event dates. Each required web searches, date verification, and backtest runs. Total: ~15 events tested per category = 75 events manually curated.
potential_fix: Build an EDGAR 8-K event scanner for each category: fallen angels (8-K with rating agency mentions), profit warnings (8-K Item 7.01 with 'guidance' in text), secondary offerings (S-3 registration statements), dividend cuts (8-K Item 8.01 with 'dividend' + 'reduced' or 'suspended'). This would give systematic event lists vs manual curation.

description: SPAC delisted tickers (VTIQ, SHLL, AJAX) - no data from yfinance or Tiingo. 3 of 8 SPAC events unmeasurable.
potential_fix: Need Polygon.io historical data for pre-2021 delisted SPAC tickers

description: S&P Global press release RSS feed returns 403 to automated requests. Cannot programmatically detect S&P 500 rebalance announcements.
potential_fix: Monitor manually on first Friday of March/June/Sep/Dec, or use Wikipedia API which lags by ~1 day

description: March 2026 S&P 500 additions (VRT, LITE, COHR, SATS) were not in our watchlist - missed trade opportunity worth ~5% abnormal return per position.
potential_fix: Built sp500_change_scanner.py and updated watchlist. Need to set calendar reminder for June 5, 2026 to run scanner.

description: largecap_filter.py parameter min_market_cap_m is in millions but name is ambiguous. Passed 500_000_000 (thinking it was raw dollars) instead of 500 (millions). Caused 100% event exclusion. Only caught because the 0/489 output was obviously wrong.
potential_fix: Rename parameter to min_market_cap_millions or add a docstring example showing the correct unit, or add an assertion that raises ValueError if value > 1_000_000 (impossible market cap in millions).

description: Clinical failure scanner requires manual review of 8-K text to determine if failure is new vs previously disclosed. 10 filings took significant time to classify. Auto-classification of first disclosure vs follow-up filing would save time.
potential_fix: Add a check: if the 8-K references a previous date for the failure announcement, flag as follow-up and deprioritize.

description: edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py guesses exhibit filenames (ex99-1.htm, ex991.htm, etc.) rather than parsing the EDGAR filing index first. Results in 404 errors when issuers use non-standard names (e.g. ALDX used 'aldx-ex99_1.htm', RLMD used 'ea028268801ex99-1.htm'). Fix: fetch the filing index page first to extract the actual exhibit URL, then fetch the exhibit text.
potential_fix: In edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py, after getting the accession number, fetch https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&CIK={cik}&type=8-K&dateb=&owner=include&count=10 or the filing index at https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{accession_nodash}/index.json to get the actual exhibit filename list.

description: VWAP momentum: Small-universe finding (+0.64-1.19%) did not replicate with 100-stock universe N=14,412. Required full re-test to discover. Need to standardize minimum universe size for characteristic-based backtests (at least 50 stocks, N>5000 observations).
potential_fix: Add minimum universe size check to characteristic-based backtest scripts. Consider a standard universe file (100 S&P 500 stocks) to use consistently across all tests.

description: measure_event_impact() with per-event entry_price='open' override returns entry_price_mode='close' in results. Unclear if per-event override actually applies or if summary stat shows only one mode. Hard to audit.
potential_fix: Add per-event entry_price_type field to individual_impacts output so each event's actual entry method is visible in the summary.

description: EDGAR EFTS search URL built manually with requests.utils.quote causes HTTP 500; must use requests.get(url, params=params, ...) format. This wasted 2 turns debugging.
potential_fix: Standardize EDGAR search function across all tools to use params= argument, not manual URL construction. See edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py search_edgar() as the gold standard.

description: GPS (Gap Inc.) has no data in yfinance (returns 404) or Tiingo (returns empty). Ticker exists on NYSE but yfinance can't find it. Lost 1 event from backtest.
potential_fix: Check alternative ticker variants (GPS, GPS.N) or use Bloomberg/Polygon for tickers that yfinance misses.

description: research.create_hypothesis() expects historical_evidence as LIST of event dicts, not a summary dict. CLAUDE.md example shows dict format which is wrong. Caused ValueError on first attempt.
potential_fix: Update CLAUDE.md example to show list-of-dicts format for historical_evidence.

description: No clean API for identifying 'first-ever' buyback announcements. EDGAR EFTS entity filter returns all matching docs (10000 cap) not filtered by CIK. Had to check filing text manually for each company. Many first-ever buybacks co-announced with earnings (items 2.02+8.01) making clean isolation very difficult. Community banks frequently have unverifiable prior buyback history due to pre-EDGAR full-text era. Took 20+ API calls per company to verify 'first-ever' status.
potential_fix: Build a tool that checks company 8-K + 10-K full text for prior repurchase mentions, caches results, and batches the verification. A 'first_ever_buyback_verifier.py' tool would save 10+ turns in future sessions.

description: CEO departure signal is fully validated (p=0.0034, N=13, avg -3.25% abnormal) but untradeable because trader.py has no after-hours order support. The signal fires in the overnight gap. Need to add extended_hours=True to Alpaca order API calls and test AH short selling.
potential_fix: Add after_hours=True parameter to trader.py Alpaca order submission. Alpaca supports extended hours trading. Test with paper account first.

description: First-ever buyback EDGAR search contamination: 8-K items 2.02 (earnings) and 8.01 (buyback) often filed same day, requiring manual disambiguation. Could not reliably scale beyond N=8 clean events.
potential_fix: Parse 8-K item numbers to filter Item 8.01 (repurchase) from same-day Item 2.02 (earnings) automatically.

description: EDGAR CEO departure background scanner has very high false positive rate. Of 28 large-cap flagged events: 27 disqualified (CFO changes, board additions, divisional reshuffles, bylaw amendments, planned retirements). Only 1 genuine performance-failure departure (GPOR) with window already closed. Scanner needs better filtering: (1) parse 8-K text for CEO vs CFO distinction, (2) check for negative stock reaction as signal, (3) exclude Item 5.02 where departure language is clearly planned/succession.
potential_fix: Add title parsing to CEO scanner: require filing text to contain [Chief Executive] or [CEO] departure, exclude [CFO, COO, board, director, President of]. Also add pre-filter: stock must have dropped >2% on the event date.

description: GPOR (Gulfport Energy) CEO John Reinhart abrupt resignation March 9, 2026 - stock dropped 7.0%. Missed trade entry window (9+ trading days past). Would have been a valid hypothesis 5dbcfb37 trade if detected at announcement. Another validation point for the overnight-gap CEO departure signal.
potential_fix: Background scanner should alert in real-time (within 2 hours of 8-K filing), not batch-process at session end.

description: add_research_task() priority parameter expects integer 1-5 but docstring doesn't make this clear. Used string 'high' which caused TypeError in queue sort. Would benefit from input validation and clearer docs.
potential_fix: Add input validation in research_queue.add_research_task(): if isinstance(priority, str): convert or raise helpful error. Update docstring.

description: realtime_insider_scanner.py timed out/killed for 3rd confirmed time. Parsing 500 EDGAR CIKs sequentially takes ~6 min minimum — always exceeds task timeout. OpenInsider scraper is the practical replacement for fresh cluster detection. Scanner provides no unique value over OpenInsider for clusters filed in the last 30 days.
potential_fix: Either (1) cap scan to 50 most-recent CIKs only, (2) use asyncio/concurrent requests with rate limiting, or (3) retire the tool entirely in favor of openinsider_scraper.py — label it deprecated in the header comment.

description: No automated outcome-tracking for missed clusters. Had to manually run measure_event_impact() for POOL, AMRZ, and AHCO to check if signals worked. A tool that auto-records outcomes for watchlisted missed opportunities would save 3-4 turns per session.
potential_fix: Add missed_opportunity watchlist entries to research_queue and build a nightly outcome-checker script that measures abnormal returns and records them to knowledge base automatically.

description: clinical_failure_short hypothesis needs manual assessment of regulatory path forward qualifier before each trade - requires reading press release carefully for subgroup data and FDA meeting plans
potential_fix: Add a pre-trade checklist function that prompts researcher to answer 3 binary questions before activating clinical failure short

description: set_next_session_priorities(handoff=...) expects a dict but CLAUDE.md implies it accepts a string. Caused TypeError. Docstring is misleading - shows example with keys but does not document that a plain string will fail.
potential_fix: Either (1) accept both str and dict in research_queue.set_next_session_priorities by wrapping strings as {"summary": handoff}, or (2) update CLAUDE.md to show the dict format explicitly.

description: measure_event_impact() re-runs give different results than stored hypothesis historical_evidence values (e.g., WD 117 events: stored mean_1d=+1.63% but re-run gives -0.30%). The stored values were computed with different benchmarking/entry logic. Need to use stored pre-computed values for regime analysis rather than re-running, or document which computation is authoritative.
potential_fix: Add documentation noting that historical_evidence stores original backtest values and re-running measure_event_impact on the same events may differ due to data updates, timing, or benchmark changes. Regime analysis should use stored values when available.

description: Previous regime analysis on smaller dataset incorrectly identified elevated VIX as best regime due to 2023 sample bias. Full-population analysis (N=1566) corrected this: calm VIX is dramatically better. Context loss between sessions allowed this incorrect finding to persist.
potential_fix: When recording known effects, note the sample size and whether the finding could be reversed with more data.

description: 2021 regime CSV result (-0.51%) appeared to contradict original backtest (+2.93%). Spent 4 turns investigating. Turned out original backtest covered 2020+2021 combined, not 2021 alone. The 2020 COVID recovery was included. Need to always document whether a backtest period is a single year or range.
potential_fix: When recording OOS split results, always note the exact year range, not just "discovery" vs "validation". Include the N per year breakdown in the hypothesis record.

description: n>=10 cluster signal disappeared entirely (N=155, p=0.96). Counter-intuitive: more insiders should = stronger signal. Possible causes: (1) company-wide insider plans at large caps (10b5-1), (2) selection bias toward large caps where insider signal is weakest, (3) noise from employee stock purchase plans. Would benefit from dedicated investigation.
potential_fix: Add n_insiders range 6-9 as optimal tier to scanner. Test explicitly whether n=6-9 outperforms n>=10 on a statistically sound basis.

description: cluster_with_returns.csv only has 5d returns -- cannot check multi-horizon passes_multiple_testing for n=6-9 tier without re-running full measure_event_impact(). Had to defer n=6-9 hypothesis pre-registration to next session.
potential_fix: When running cluster detector, always save 1d,3d,5d,10d,20d returns to cluster_with_returns.csv so any sub-group analysis can check multi-horizon significance without re-running expensive API calls.

description: create_hypothesis() requires historical_evidence as a list of per-event dicts (symbol, date, abnormal_1d, abnormal_5d, ...), not a summary stats dict. This is not documented in CLAUDE.md API quick reference. Wasted 2 turns debugging ValueError before checking code.
potential_fix: Add to CLAUDE.md quick reference: historical_evidence must be a list of per-event dicts matching individual_impacts format from measure_event_impact(). Alternatively add a summary-dict input path in research.py that auto-builds placeholder event list.

description: Tiingo API returns 429 Too Many Requests when running a second full backtest (437 events) immediately after the first. Rate limit hit causes second script to fail mid-run, requiring workaround using saved numbers from first run.
potential_fix: Add a sleep/retry wrapper in market_data.py for Tiingo 429 errors. Or cache backtest results to disk (pickle) so re-runs within the same session skip API calls.

description: create_hypothesis() with validation_indices=[10, 11] (only 2 items) succeeded even though CLAUDE.md says min 3. Either the enforcement is not strict or the check was removed. Unclear requirement.
potential_fix: Clarify whether validation_indices minimum is enforced in code or just documentation. If enforced, add clearer error message.

description: create_hypothesis() did not enforce min validation_indices=3 at pre-registration time for hypotheses 9f8a4c61 and 7b5d0120. Both were created with fewer than 3 real named validation instances. The compliance gate should have caught this.
potential_fix: Add assertion in create_hypothesis() code that len(out_of_sample_split.validation_indices) >= 3 before saving hypothesis. Also validate that validation indices resolve to real (non-aggregate) events.

description: S&P 500 March 2026 rebalance was announced March 6 but not caught until March 22 (effective March 23). There is no automated monitoring of S&P Global index announcements. Missed 16-day window to trade VRT, LITE, COHR, SATS additions under hypothesis 061ae3a8.
potential_fix: Add spglobal.com/spdji RSS feed or weekly web search for S&P 500 announcement to cluster_auto_scanner.py or a new sp500_announcement_monitor.py. Schedule it weekly via launchd.

description: Missed S&P 500 addition trade: VRT, LITE, COHR, SATS announced March 6 (effective March 23). Hypothesis 061ae3a8 was pre-registered and ready, but no monitoring was in place to catch the announcement. Built sp500_announcement_scanner.py to prevent future misses. 16-day trading window was available and completely missed.
potential_fix: sp500_announcement_scanner.py built and scheduled via launchd. Should run daily M-F at 9am ET going forward.

description: Trigger-setting process has no automatic VIX tier validation. Previous session set WD trigger (n=3) in same session that established Tier 3 = n>=6 only rule. The trigger setter did not cross-check against VIX tier at time of setting. Need a validate_trigger_against_vix_rule() check before any trigger is set.
potential_fix: Add VIX tier check to cluster_auto_scanner.py set_cluster_trigger() function and any manual trigger-setting workflow. Always call get_vix_action_recommendation(vix_regime, n_insiders, total_value_k) before setting trigger and refuse if returns DO_NOT_TRADE.

description: edgar_ceo_departure_scanner.py checks filing_date for overnight gap but measures close-to-close not open-to-open. ATR showed as -4.1% but the actual overnight gap was -3.9% (correct direction). CPT showed as -2.3% but the actual filing-day gap was near zero. The timing calculation for when a CEO departure is announced (pre-market, during hours, after hours) is not captured.
potential_fix: Add after-hours announcement detection: if filing_date is after 4pm ET, the 'next day' open should be checked. If during hours, the same-day close should be checked. Use SEC filing timestamp (not just date) to determine announcement timing.

description: n=6-9 cluster full-population discovery failure was a new finding this session that required re-running expensive backtests (3 separate backtest runs). The hypothesis 416556df was pre-registered without this full-population validation. Better practice: always run full-population backtest BEFORE pre-registering, not after.
potential_fix: Add methodology note: for cluster-tier hypotheses, require full-population temporal split backtest as part of pre-registration verification, not just the named 16-event sample.

description: edgar_ceo_departure_scanner.py check_stock_direction failed for ALL tickers due to yfinance MultiIndex columns. Previous session added _scalar() helper but it did not handle all cases. Fixed by flattening columns immediately after download with get_level_values(0). This bug caused FBIN CEO departure (March 16) to be missed as a trading signal.
potential_fix: Fixed. All yfinance downloads should flatten MultiIndex columns immediately after download. Add to coding guidelines for this project.

description: EDGAR EFTS search for 'did not meet primary endpoint' returns 100 hits per year but requires manual price verification for each - now automated with --auto-qualify in edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py.
potential_fix: RESOLVED: --auto-qualify flag added to scanner.

description: ceo_departure_alerts.jsonl contained stale entries (BG, RIVN, SMA, CPT from March 19) that appeared actionable at session start. The entries were false positives from the scanner but had no expiry timestamp. Wasted 2 turns verifying they were stale.
potential_fix: Add expiry check to ceo_departure_alerts.jsonl entries or clear the file after each session start. The scanner already has 2-day window check but old entries persist.

description: Missed Q1 2026 S&P 500 rebalance trade (VRT/LITE/COHR/SATS, +5.20% avg abnormal 1d). Watchlist only had June 2026 entry when hypothesis was created on 2026-03-19. All four quarterly dates (Mar/Jun/Sep/Dec) should have been added when the hypothesis was created. Fix: added September and December quarterly watchlist entries now.
potential_fix: When creating index-addition hypothesis, automatically generate all four quarterly watchlist entries for the current year. Consider adding a cron job to check spglobal.com/press-releases the first Friday of each quarter-end month (Mar/Jun/Sep/Dec).

description: SEO backtest: 424B4 filing date lags true announcement by 0-7 days (median 3 days). Cannot use 424B4 as clean event date without reading 8-K content. Need commercial data (CRSP SEO dataset) or real-time 8-K monitoring for the announcement-day signal.
potential_fix: Build 8-K item 8.01 scanner that reads EDGAR submissions API to find exact announcement dates. Or use secondary offering databases (Bloomberg, CRSP) if API access obtained.

description: EDGAR form.idx fixed-width format has variable padding between header and data lines. Column header positions (12, 74, 86, 98) do not match actual data offsets. Regex parsing required: r"^(\S+)\s+(.*?)\s{2,}(\d+)\s+(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})\s+(edgar/\S+)". Fixed-offset slicing produces wrong dates.
potential_fix: Always use regex for form.idx. Documented in seo_bought_deal_scanner.py. Add note to CLAUDE.md custom tools section.

description: OpenInsider cluster screener counts RSU award grants (Form 4 code A) as buying events, inflating cluster count. POOL showed 3x but only 1 was open-market purchase. Requires EDGAR Form 4 XML verification for every cluster before trading.
potential_fix: Add EDGAR Form 4 XML verification step to cluster_auto_scanner.py: for each cluster, pull Form 4 filings and count only transactions with code=P and acq=A (open-market purchases).

description: VIX gate from cluster_auto_scanner.py not automatically applied to manual trade setups in research sessions. Set WD trigger manually without checking VIX gate - had to manually correct.
potential_fix: Add VIX gate check to hypothesis trigger-setting code or create a validation helper function that checks VIX level against cluster size before allowing trigger.

description: EDGAR Form 4 XML fetching requires 2 HTTP requests per filing (folder listing + XML) at 0.08s sleep each. For 25 Form 4 filings per cluster check, this adds ~4s per cluster. With 3-5 clusters checked per session, adds ~15-20s total but is acceptable given the correctness benefit.
potential_fix: Cache CIK lookups (already done with _cik_cache). Could cache per-ticker Form 4 results for 24h to avoid re-fetching on same-day runs.

description: EDGAR bulk quarterly Form 4 data (REPORTINGOWNER.RPTOWNER_TITLE) is missing for 39.5% of clusters. The bulk TSV files sometimes don't include RPTOWNER_TITLE even when the XML filing has it. This forced using price-cached tickers only and introduced misclassification of CEO/CFO clusters. Discovered the real-time Form 4 XML has <officerTitle> field which IS reliable but requires per-filing HTTP requests.
potential_fix: For live scanner use the Form 4 XML officerTitle field (already done in EDGAR XML parsing). For historical analysis, could re-extract titles from cached Form 4 XMLs (more accurate than the bulk TSV title field). Would increase title coverage from 60.5% to ~90%+.

description: EDGAR EFTS search for first quarterly cash dividend returns 52 results but entity_name field is missing -- requires display_names regex parsing. Could not use largecap_filter since it expects DataFrame not list. Had to manually check market caps via yfinance.
potential_fix: Build a utility function that takes EDGAR EFTS hits and returns (ticker, date, entity_name) tuples with market cap already filtered.

description: ALDX was already disqualified in a previous session (bounce after crash) but appeared in auto-qualify scan again this session. The scanner does not have memory of previously evaluated events. Spent 3 turns re-verifying.
potential_fix: Add a "previously_evaluated" cache to edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py. Events that were already manually disqualified should be skipped in future scans.

description: pre_earnings_runup.py initial version had bug: earnings dates have time component (16:00:00) but price index is date-only. pd.DataFrame comparison fails silently, returning 0 events. Required debugging pass.
potential_fix: Always normalize datetime index with .dt.normalize() when merging earnings dates with price data.

description: clusters_with_roles.csv only covers 290/1566 (18.5%) of return events, severely limiting 3-way analysis. The role data came from a separate analysis run with different date coverage. The n=6-9 tier has only 38 merged events vs 353 in the full returns dataset.
potential_fix: Re-run the CEO/CFO role classifier (from tools/insider_cluster_detector.py or a new script) on ALL events in cluster_with_returns.csv. This would enable proper 3-way analysis with full coverage.

description: clusters_with_roles.csv (18.5% join coverage) was built from different event detection run than cluster_with_returns.csv - ticker+date join failed for 81.5% of events. Required building new tool (build_roles_from_bulk.py) to re-match from raw bulk data.
potential_fix: Always store role data keyed to the same event IDs used in returns data. The build_roles_from_bulk.py tool now solves this.

description: ALDX re-appeared in clinical scanner again (third time). No persistent disqualification memory prevents re-checking already-known events.
potential_fix: Add disqualified_tickers list to clinical failure scanner state file with reason and date.

description: EDGAR EFTS PDUFA calendar search: web search and fetch attempts for Q2 2026 PDUFA dates returned CSS-only pages or 403 errors from MarketBeat, BiopharmaWatch, BiopharmCatalyst. Only biomednexus.com returned usable data (April-June 2026 partial list). BioPharma calendar data is largely paywalled.
potential_fix: Build EDGAR-based PDUFA scanner using open.fda.gov API (transparency/crl.json already used). Supplement with free tier of BiopharmIQ or build scraper for RTTNews FDA calendar which is publicly accessible.

description: realtime_insider_scanner.py launched in background but returned no output. Tool may be blocking on network request. cluster_auto_scanner.py and openinsider_scraper.py are more reliable for cluster detection.
potential_fix: Add timeout and error handling to realtime_insider_scanner.py. Consider deprecating it in favor of cluster_auto_scanner.py which is more reliable.

description: CEO departure scanner generates false positives: (1) CVBF 8-K was merger proxy supplement (Item 8.01), not an officer departure (Item 5.02). Scanner triggered on officer compensation language in the proxy. (2) SDRL classified as "performance_failure" due to "effective immediately" but was actually an orderly internal CCO-to-CEO promotion with outperforming stock (+72% YoY). Manual review required 2 turns of web research to confirm both were false positives. Fix: scanner should verify Item 5.02 is in the 8-K exhibit index before classifying, AND check 12-month sector relative performance.
potential_fix: In edgar_ceo_departure_scanner.py: (1) parse filing index to confirm Item 5.02 is listed before fetching full text; (2) after performance_failure classification, check yfinance for 12-month stock vs sector performance — disqualify if stock outperformed sector.

description: Analyst upgrade/downgrade data from yfinance only available end-of-day (no intraday timestamps precise enough to know if note was before or after market). This means we can only enter at next-day open, fully missing any premarket price reaction. The analyst downgrade signal has real statistical power (p<0.0001) but the edge is consumed by costs when entering at open. A pre-market data feed would likely make this tradeable.
potential_fix: Subscribe to a real-time analyst recommendation feed (Bloomberg, Refinitiv, or AlphaSense) that provides note release timestamps. Then test entry at premarket print vs. at open.

description: FINRA bimonthly short interest data (the academic standard from Asquith et al 2005) is not freely downloadable in bulk. NASDAQ API provides 24 settlement periods (~12 months) for NASDAQ-listed stocks only. NYSE-listed stocks require different source. 12 months is insufficient for proper discovery/validation temporal split (need at least 2+ years). FINRA REGSHO daily short volume is available going back to 2020 but is flow data (not position data) and requires aggregation to compute short interest.
potential_fix: Use FINRA REGSHO daily short volume ratio (ShortVolume/TotalVolume) as a proxy for short interest buildup. Build rolling 20-day window and detect spikes >2 sigma above rolling mean. This is actually related to Boehmer et al (2008) who found daily short volume is predictive. Alternatively, try OpenShorts.io or Quandl free tier for historical bimonthly short interest.

description: Tiingo API returns 429 errors when running consecutive large backtests (400-500 events each). Each backtest triggers many Tiingo fallback calls for delisted tickers. Second backtest in same session hits rate limit causing ~10-15% data loss.
potential_fix: Add exponential backoff retry in market_data.py for 429 errors. Or add sleep(0.5) between Tiingo API calls. Or cache Tiingo results to disk keyed by (ticker, start_date, end_date) to avoid repeated API calls within same session.

description: pre_earnings_runup.py run_pre_earnings_analysis() returns raw events list + summary dict but printed to stdout mixed with progress messages. Parsing results from stdout is error-prone. Should save structured results to disk automatically.
potential_fix: Add --output flag to pre_earnings_runup.py to save structured results to JSON file. Separate progress logging from results output.

description: set_next_session_priorities() handoff parameter requires a dict, not a string. CLAUDE.md API reference shows it takes a dict but gives no example. Wasted 2 turns debugging.
potential_fix: Update CLAUDE.md to add an example showing handoff={...} dict structure.

description: Market cap lookup for 2680 large-cap tickers required iterating yfinance one by one - only processed 500 tickers in batch. The largecap_filter.py tool exists but relies on cached data from a prior run, not a live batch lookup.
potential_fix: Pre-build a comprehensive large-cap ticker list (e.g. S&P 500 + Russell 1000 components) that stays static and doesn't require per-session API calls.

description: fiftytwo_week_low_scanner.py can only set one trigger at a time. When 2+ signals fire simultaneously (ABT and HD on March 20), each overwrites expected_symbol on the same hypothesis record. Last write wins (HD). ABT trigger was silently lost. Design requires one hypothesis record per active signal.
potential_fix: Scanner should check if hypothesis already has an active trigger and create a separate trade record, OR use a signal queue approach, OR the hypothesis should support multiple concurrent symbols.

description: Running fiftytwo_week_low_scanner.py interactively (outside scheduled hours) during a status-check session inadvertently modified live hypothesis state. Scanner is designed to run as a cronjob but has no guard against interactive use. Called it to check for signals per task instructions but it actually set live triggers.
potential_fix: Add --dry-run as default mode when run outside scheduled window. Or add a guard: if trigger already set and matches current signal, skip without modifying.

description: yfinance intraday data (5m intervals) only available for recent 1-day period, and appears to only have data through March 20 (last trading day) not March 23 real-time. Cannot confirm VIX level today before market open. Had to rely on March 20 close as last known value.
potential_fix: Use Alpaca market data API for real-time quotes on ^VIX or VXX as proxy. Or accept that VIX is checked at session time (which may be pre-market).

description: March 23 market data not available at session time (Sunday 7:17 PM ET). Tasks requiring today open price cannot be completed until market opens Monday.
potential_fix: Schedule sessions after market close (4:30+ PM ET on trading days) to ensure same-day data is available

description: openinsider_scraper.py uses --days flag not --lookback. Task instructions used wrong flag name.
potential_fix: Standardize all scanner tools to use --days flag or add --lookback as alias

description: 52w-low scan in task instructions used rolling(252).min() on 1y history which produces NaN for last row. Should use 2y history or direct .tail(252).min()
potential_fix: Document correct pattern in CLAUDE.md quick reference for 52w-low computation

description: NKE is 2.7% above 52w low and BAX is only 0.4% above - both should be caught automatically by the scanner. Verified scanner is scheduled via launchd at 9:15 PM ET weekdays. No manual action needed but should verify launchd plist is active after any system restarts.
potential_fix: Verify launchd plist is still active after system restarts: launchctl list | grep financial

description: insider_cluster_events.csv from worktree has many delisted tickers (100% failure rate on initial sample). Need to pre-filter to active large-caps before sampling.
potential_fix: Add a 'yfinance_accessible' flag to cluster datasets, or pre-filter using largecap_filter.py before storing cluster data.

description: yfinance multi-level columns for single-ticker downloads requires .squeeze() call. All scripts that use hist['Close'].iloc[n] fail if they try float() conversion without squeeze.
potential_fix: Create a yfinance helper function get_close_series(ticker, start, end) that always returns a clean Series, handling multi-level columns automatically. Add to yfinance_utils.py.

description: 52w-low scanner checks only 66 hardcoded tickers. BAX (at 52w low, $8.31B) is not in the universe and won't be auto-detected. Need broader universe or dynamic S&P 500 membership check.
potential_fix: Pull S&P 500 membership list from Wikipedia or EDGAR at scanner startup, rather than using hardcoded ticker list. Update fiftytwo_week_low_scanner.py to use dynamic universe.

description: measure_event_impact() returns '?' string (not float) for horizons with insufficient data. Caused ValueError when trying to format with :.2f%. Must check type before formatting or use simple {} format.
potential_fix: Add a safe_fmt helper that returns '?' if value is string, else formats as float. Or update measure_event_impact() to return NaN instead of '?' string.

description: EDGAR full-text search API (efts.sec.gov) returns 0 hits for exact ASR phrase 'accelerated share repurchase'. Had to use different search terms and cross-validate with 8-K EDGAR index. Adds friction to ASR event discovery.
potential_fix: Build a dedicated ASR scanner that searches for 8-K filings mentioning 'accelerated share repurchase' using SEC full-text search with broader terms and save to data/ cache.

description: Scanner gap for V (Visa) — 52w low touch March 18 was missed because universe was hardcoded 66 tickers without V/MA/COF. V is a $440B company that clearly belongs. Gap discovered only because session happened to check PDUFA and incidentally looked at V chart.
potential_fix: Add a coverage audit at session start: for each hypothesis with a backtest_symbols list, verify those symbols are in the corresponding scanner universe. Flag mismatches.

description: measure_event_impact() result has p_value_abnormal_{h} keys, not p_abnormal_{h}. ValueError when trying to format as float because measure_event_impact returns ? string for missing horizons.
potential_fix: Add a safe_fmt() helper in CLAUDE.md or create a results_formatter.py utility that handles both ? strings and floats gracefully.

description: Wikipedia S&P 500 table blocked by 403 (HTTP). Cannot dynamically fetch S&P 500 components for scanner universe expansion. Had to build manual list.
potential_fix: Build a cached sp500_universe.json file that is updated quarterly via EDGAR company search or SEC official list. Avoid Wikipedia dependency.

description: Background analysis produced n=107 tech beats result but I had no access to the actual event list used. Had to reconstruct from scratch using FAANG stocks. If the background analysis script stored its event list, I could have directly replicated and done temporal splits on the exact same data.
potential_fix: Background analysis scripts should serialize their event lists to a temp file or log for cross-session replication. Add event_list storage to any batch analysis scripts.

description: EDGAR EFTS search-index API consistently returns empty entity_name and ticker fields in _source, showing '?' for all results. Cannot get company names/tickers from EFTS API - only filing dates are returned. Affects all research requiring EDGAR event list building.
potential_fix: Use EDGAR company search (data.sec.gov/cik-lookup-data.txt) to build CIK-to-ticker mapping, then join EFTS results by accession number. Or use the EDGAR full-index files (quarter/*.idx) to get ticker-to-filing mappings.

description: yfinance earnings_dates only returns UPCOMING quarters (next 4 quarters), not historical earnings dates. Cannot test insider cluster proximity to historical earnings (2021-2024 clusters need 2021-2024 earnings dates). Historical earnings schedule not available from yfinance.
potential_fix: Use EDGAR 10-Q and 10-K filing dates as proxy for historical earnings release dates. Or subscribe to earnings calendar API (AlphaVantage, Polygon.io free tier) that provides historical earnings dates.

description: Spin-off EDGAR 8-K filing dates lag actual press release announcement dates by days to weeks. IBM announced Kyndryl Oct 7 AH (stock gapped +5.47% Oct 8), but 8-K filed Dec 16. EDGAR dates are useless for spin-off backtesting without Bloomberg/Refinitiv.
potential_fix: Subscribe to Bloomberg Event API or Refinitiv News API for correct announcement timestamps. Alternatively, build scraper for PR Newswire/Business Wire headlines with timestamps.

description: measure_event_impact() with entry_price='open' correctly enters at next-day open for after_hours events. This means overnight gap returns (close→open) are NOT captured - we enter at the already-gapped price. This is by design but confusing when academic papers report close-to-close returns that include the overnight gap.
potential_fix: Add entry_price='prev_close' option to measure_event_impact() to capture overnight gap. Or add a gap_analysis() helper that decomposes returns into gap vs intraday components.

description: EDGAR EFTS company_tickers.json only covers 6002-8078 exchange-listed entities. Most small/mid-cap issuers that do SEOs, spinoffs, or activist targets are not in this file. CIK-to-ticker match rate is <2% for typical 8-K search results. Bottleneck for any research requiring automated event collection for these company types.
potential_fix: Use SEC company_facts API with full-text CIK lookup. Or build a crawler that indexes all CIK->ticker mappings from SEC EDGAR full-index quarterly files. EDGAR quarterly index (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/full-index/YYYY/QTRN/company.gz) has comprehensive CIK-ticker-company mapping.

description: Activist 13D research entirely blocked by entity_name=None in EDGAR EFTS search API. Cannot identify target company from SC 13D filing search results. Requires either Bloomberg/Refinitiv for news dates, or parsing individual filing documents to extract target company name.
potential_fix: Build CIK-based target company extractor: for each SC 13D accession number, fetch the index JSON from SEC, find the primary document, extract target company name from the filing header (SUBJECT COMPANY field in sgml header).

description: pre_earnings_runup.py existed but used raw yf.download (no MultiIndex fix) and had display bug (positive_rate showed as '5050%' instead of '50.5%' because raw dict value is 50.5 not 0.505). Script ran fine once I read the raw dict directly.
potential_fix: Update pre_earnings_runup.py print formatting to not multiply positive_rate by 100 (it's already a percentage, not a decimal).

description: RESOLVED: EDGAR EFTS display_names field contains ticker in parentheses (format: "Company (TICK) (CIK XXX)"). Previous attempts used entity_name which is always null. Fix: use display_names[0] and parse first (ALPHA) match. Unlocks all EDGAR-based event research without Bloomberg/Refinitiv.
potential_fix: FIXED - use display_names field in EDGAR EFTS API responses. See known_effects[edgar_efts_display_names_fix] for full details.

description: REPL research task was listed as 2023 CRL but actual CRL was July 2025. Previous session incorrectly noted the date without verifying. Web research required 1 turn to fix.
potential_fix: When adding research tasks, always include the exact date found (not an estimate). Use web search upfront.

description: RESOLVED: EDGAR entity_name always null. display_names field has tickers. Format: Company (TICK) (CIK). Fix documented in CLAUDE.md.
potential_fix: FIXED - see CLAUDE.md EDGAR EFTS API section

description: EDGAR EFTS pagination returns max ~500-1000 hits but there are 6441 total 'strategic alternatives' 8-K filings. Cannot get exhaustive sample - only first 1000. This means we may miss relevant large-cap events in lower-ranked pages. No way to sort by market cap in EFTS API.
potential_fix: Use EDGAR full-index quarterly files to get ALL 8-K filings, then text-match for 'strategic alternatives' in filing header. This gives complete coverage but requires downloading large index files (~500MB/quarter).

description: Russell 2000 reconstitution signal testing blocked by lack of free constituent change lists. FTSE Russell publishes preliminary lists but these are not archived in machine-readable format. EDGAR 8-K search only finds ~15/year self-announcing microcaps, not the full 200+ additions. Paid data (CRSP, Bloomberg, or FTSE Russell data license) required for complete event dataset.
potential_fix: Subscribe to CRSP or Bloomberg Russell constituent data. Alternatively, build a web scraper for FTSE Russell historical membership files (if publicly archived) or use iShares IWM daily holdings snapshots near reconstitution effective dates to identify new additions by diffing consecutive holdings files.

description: EDGAR full-text search for credit upgrades returned too many false positives (earnings calls mentioning 'investment grade' in passing). Had to manually verify each event via web search to confirm true junk-to-IG upgrade. Rating agency press releases (Moody's, S&P, Fitch) are not in EDGAR, making systematic discovery difficult.
potential_fix: Build a tool that fetches from a credit rating database or parses Moody's/S&P RSS feeds. Alternatively, scrape bondblox.com or cbonds.com which track rating changes systematically.

description: Credit upgrade event dates require individual web searches per company. No free systematic API for all junk-to-IG upgrades. Bloomberg/Moody's/S&P have comprehensive databases but are paywalled. BondBlox.com has some events but not comprehensive. Had to do ~15 individual web searches to find 12 clean events. Took ~45 minutes to expand from n=9 to n=12.
potential_fix: Build a BondBlox scraper or EDGAR 8-K keyword scraper for 'upgraded to investment grade'. Companies often issue 8-K (or 8-K/A) when receiving IG upgrade. Search EDGAR EFTS for 'investment grade' 'rating' in 8-K filings 2015-2024 to build systematic dataset.

description: Credit rating upgrade exact dates often unavailable without Bloomberg subscription. PR Fitch first IG (July 2025) could not be precisely dated despite multiple web searches - BondBlox and Investing.com both behind paywall or 403 errors. Critical for hypothesis backtesting. Workaround: tried probing July dates but signal too noisy without anchor date.
potential_fix: Build EDGAR 8-K scanner specifically for credit rating upgrade announcements: many companies file 8-K when receiving IG upgrade (Permian Resources filed March 17 2026 8-K for S&P upgrade). Systematic EDGAR EFTS search for 'investment grade' 'upgrade' in 8-K filings with date range filter would capture most first IG events at announcement date.

description: Rating agency coverage start dates affect hypothesis qualification: Fitch only started covering CCL in Q3 2024 (at BB+). This is impossible to verify without Fitch archives. Had to rely on inference from web search results. Same issue with other companies - determining if Oct 2025 Fitch BBB- was 'first ever from Fitch' or 'regain' required multiple searches.
potential_fix: Build a credit_rating_history.json cache: when we verify a company's first IG date, log it with source URL and agency. Prevents re-investigation next session.

description: MSCI 2024-05-13 was misclassified as 'first IG' in hypothesis f86dcb4e. Moody's Baa3 was actually the THIRD agency upgrade (Fitch first ~2021, S&P second Feb 2023). Finding: Moody's MSCI press release explicitly says 'third major credit rating agency'. Cannot verify exact first-IG dates for all hypothesis events without checking all 3 agencies. Need a systematic check of every event in first-IG dataset to confirm agency order.
potential_fix: Build a tool that fetches all 3 agencies' rating histories for any company and determines the true first-IG date. Could use cbonds.com or Moody's public data.

description: Trade status investigation: status showed 0 active experiments even though GO and HD had next_market_open triggers. Root cause: market was closed at 3:25 AM ET. trade_loop.py only fires next_market_open within 5 minutes of 9:30 ET open. No trades are missing or stuck.
potential_fix: Could add a note to run.py --status showing pending triggers with expected fire time to reduce confusion.

description: realtime_insider_scanner.py timeout fixed: added --max-check N flag (default changed from 500 to 100) and reduced sleep intervals from 0.35s to 0.3s. Also added --quiet flag for automated scripts. Previously timed out 3 times wasting 8 turns.
potential_fix: RESOLVED: added --max-check flag (default 100), --quiet flag, reduced sleep to 0.3s

description: Russell 2000 reconstitution event dataset has fundamental data quality issues: 6 of 24 candidate events had market caps well above the R1000/R2000 breakpoint, meaning they were never actually demoted to R2000. The original dataset was built from news articles and press releases that incorrectly claimed certain large-cap stocks (CCL, RCL, OMC, NWL) were Russell 2000 additions. Market cap verification is essential before any index addition/demotion backtest.
potential_fix: Always verify market cap at rank day against confirmed breakpoint before including any stock in a Russell reconstitution event list. Added get_russell_additions() function to russell_recon_dataset.py with only verified events.

description: EDGAR full-text search for strategic alternatives returns repeat filers dominating results (XCUR: 22 filings, KA: 11 filings) making it hard to identify first announcements. Need dedup logic by ticker, taking earliest date.
potential_fix: Build a tool that queries EDGAR, groups by ticker, returns only first-occurrence dates with context for signals like strategic alternatives, CEO departure, etc.

description: largecap_filter.py requires DataFrame input with ticker column named ticker - not dict list. Wasted a turn on TypeError. Also min_market_cap vs min_market_cap_m naming inconsistency.
potential_fix: Add a wrapper that accepts list of dicts with symbol key and converts automatically, or update signature to accept both formats.

description: Strategic alternatives 8-K signal: EDGAR full-text search returns 138 filings but requires fundamental quality filter (is company in distress vs genuine auction?) that cannot be automated without balance sheet data access. Manual review needed to separate the two cases.
potential_fix: Add SEC financial data (10-K) parsing to distinguish distress vs strategic cases via debt ratios and cash burn. Or use market cap trajectory over prior 90 days as proxy.

description: realtime_insider_scanner.py still times out with --max-check 20. Tool is also deprecated (DeprecationWarning directs to openinsider_scraper.py). Root cause: sequential EDGAR HTTP calls take >60s even for 20 checks. The --max-check fix was insufficient. Real fix: fully retire this tool in favor of openinsider_scraper.py.
potential_fix: RESOLVED differently: openinsider_scraper.py is the replacement. cluster_auto_scanner.py already uses it. No need to fix realtime_insider_scanner.py further — just deprecate fully.

description: PDF content not extractable via WebFetch for academic papers (Princeton merger arb intro, NY Fed SR761, Accelerate guide, Princeton). Gets binary/encoded content. Lost multiple turns trying to fetch academic PDFs.
potential_fix: Use WebSearch to find HTML versions or SSRN abstract pages instead of direct PDF links. For key papers, search for HTML reproductions or use the SSRN abstract page which has key findings in abstract.

description: EDGAR HSR 8-K dataset is heavily contaminated with non-targets. The price-jump heuristic (>15% return in prior 90 days) designed to identify M&A targets also selects companies in sector momentum runs. No way to distinguish acquirer vs target from 8-K text alone. Need a separate deal database (SC TO-T filings, Wikipedia M&A tables, or SDC) for clean target lists.
potential_fix: Use SC TO-T (tender offer) filings from EDGAR — filed exclusively by the ACQUIRER, so display_names[1] is unambiguously the target. This gives clean target+date pairs without hand-verification.

description: FedEx IR page (investors.fedex.com/fedex-freight-spin-off) returns 403. SeekingAlpha returns 403. Key data (distribution ratio, record date, ex-date) not yet publicly available - likely in final Form 10 closer to June 1. No friction workaround found.
potential_fix: Check SEC EDGAR directly for Form 10 final filing closer to May 2026. FedEx will announce record date ~3-4 weeks before June 1.

description: Merger arbitrage target identification from EDGAR requires deal price data (not freely available) to distinguish targets from acquirers. The 15% pre-event return filter is a noisy proxy — it misidentifies momentum stocks as M&A targets.
potential_fix: SC TO-T tender offer filings identify acquirer unambiguously — display_names[1] is always the target. Use these for cleaner M&A target dataset.

description: Spinco short desirability assessment required manual GXO comparable research and sector analysis. This analysis should be documented as a checklist in the spinco tool.
potential_fix: Add desirability_score() function to spinco tools that checks: (a) sector overlap with parent shareholders, (b) whether spinco is a pure-play in a desired sector, (c) S&P 500 eligibility.

description: Prior session note stated VST had ~400% run-up but did not specify the time window. This session spent 2 turns discovering the prior note referred to 24m window, not 12m. Notes should always specify the measurement window explicitly.
potential_fix: Always include measurement window in research notes (e.g., 12m run-up, not just run-up).

description: research_queue.add_research_task() requires integer priority, not string ("medium"). This is not documented in CLAUDE.md. Caused one failed call, had to inspect existing queue to find format.
potential_fix: Update CLAUDE.md API quick reference to show priority as integer (e.g. 1=high, 2=medium, 3=low) or add string-to-int coercion in research_queue.py add_research_task().

description: Fitch rating action exact date not available via web search. Bondblox article published Oct 2 provided the best date proxy. Multiple search queries needed to determine Oct 2 vs Dec date. CBonds and Investing.com returned 403 errors.
potential_fix: Build a Fitch/Moody/S&P rating action scraper that monitors fitchratings.com or uses Bloomberg RSS for real-time alerts. Alternatively, EDGAR 8-K filings often contain credit rating disclosure within days.

description: Credit upgrade announcement timing (during-hours vs after-hours) not captured in any database. Required manual WebSearch to determine CCL upgrade announcement was during market hours. This distinction materially affects trade entry timing.
potential_fix: When building upgrade event records, always capture announcement_time field (pre-market, intraday, after-hours). Check press release timestamps from PR Newswire or Businesswire.

description: research_queue has two separate task lists: 'tasks' (used by set_next_session_priorities) and 'queue' (used by complete_research_task). complete_research_task silently fails when task is in 'tasks'. Had to manually update queue structure.
potential_fix: Merge 'tasks' and 'queue' keys into one list in research_queue.py, or make complete_research_task check both keys.

description: REPL task was listed as '2023 CRL' but actual CRL was July 2025. Task description was wrong from prior session.
potential_fix: When logging research tasks, always include year in event description to prevent date confusion.

description: 52w low detector had 1% tolerance bug — classified near-misses (NKE at -0.77% from 52w min) as genuine crosses. Required manual verification.
potential_fix: Build dedicated 52w low scanner tool with strict detection (close <= 52w_min + $0.01) and market cap filter.

description: S&P 500 full ticker list not readily available — Wikipedia read_html blocked (403), SPY holdings only have top 10. Had to use manual list of ~230 stocks.
potential_fix: Download and cache a full S&P 500 constituent list from a reliable source. Update quarterly.

description: Market was closed (6:55 AM ET) when session started, but status report said '11:54' without timezone. Wasted 2 turns figuring out machine is in CET timezone and market opens at 2:30 PM CET (9:30 AM ET).
potential_fix: Add timezone info and ET equivalent to run.py --status output. Show 'Market opens in X hours (9:30 AM ET = 2:30 PM CET)'

description: Dividend cut event dates required manual compilation from memory. EDGAR 8-K search returned mostly small-cap events; large-cap cuts (Boeing, Disney COVID-era) used different language not captured.
potential_fix: Build dividend cut collector using EDGAR 8-K with broader phrases + market cap filter. Or use news API for large-cap dividend changes.

description: Credit rating downgrade (IG->HY fallen angel) data not available on EDGAR. Agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) don't disclose via 8-K reliably. Need alternative data source.
potential_fix: Subscribe to FRED bond market data or scrape Moody's press releases. Build fallen_angel_scraper.py.

description: Tiingo API returns 429 Too Many Requests when running 11-event backtest twice in quick succession. All events require Tiingo (delisted tickers). Cannot run split-group analysis immediately after full analysis. Required 30s+ sleep between calls.
potential_fix: Cache Tiingo results to disk keyed by (ticker, start_date, end_date) with 24-hour TTL. Re-running same backtest within session would be instant. Could save in a ~/.tiingo_cache/ directory.

description: verify_event_date() parameter is threshold_pct not crash_threshold. Wasted 1 call calling with wrong param name crash_threshold. Function signature not obvious from name.
potential_fix: Add crash_threshold as an alias in verify_event_date() or update CLAUDE.md to show correct parameter name threshold_pct.

description: measure_event_impact() uses timing parameter to determine entry price. Without timing=after_hours, it uses prior-day close which captures the announcement-day spike. This caused the original N=11 merger arb result to be CORRECT (it did use after_hours+open entry) but the expanded N=20 initially used wrong timing (no timing parameter). Need to always use timing=after_hours with entry_price=open for M&A announcement backtests. Also: VG and SAIL have no data in yfinance OR Tiingo - both delisted before Tiingo coverage begins.
potential_fix: Document in CLAUDE.md: for M&A announcement backtests, always use timing=after_hours + entry_price=open. Add explicit warning in measure_event_impact() docs about the timing parameter affecting what is measured.
